Medi-Cal | Shasta County CA
Toggle Menu
Medi-Cal
Important:
Medi-Cal beneficiaries, you can update your contact information with the Shasta County office:
• By phone: 877-652-0731     • Online:
BenefitsCal
.com
• In person:
Find your local county office here
Affordable health care is essential to health and well-being. Options for health care coverage are available, depending on your individual situation.
Medi-Cal
Medi-Cal is California’s version of the federal Medicaid health coverage program. Medi-Cal provides health care coverage to California residents with low income.
Who can get Medi-Cal?
Children under age 21
Low-income families with children
Individuals ages 65 or older
Disabled or blind individuals
Individuals receiving nursing home care
Pregnant people
Low-income adults, ages 21–64, without children
What does Medi-Cal cover?
A
detailed list of covered services
is available on the California Department of Health Care Services (DHCS) website.
How to apply for Medi-Cal
Online through
BenefitsCal
or
Covered California
If your income is higher than Medi-Cal allows, health insurance plans are available through Covered California. If you apply through the Covered California website, your application will be evaluated for both Medi-Cal and Covered California plans.
By phone: Call 877-652-0731 to complete and electronically sign the Medi-Cal application
By mail: Call 877-652-0731 to request an application be mailed to you. Or, print an online application, fill out and mail to:
Shasta County Health and Human Services Agency, P.O. Box 496005, Redding CA 96049-6005
In person, by visiting one of our
local county offices
Once approved, Medi-Cal benefits are managed under
Partnership Health Plan of California
. Each Medi-Cal member will receive a Partnership ID card, an introduction letter and a Member Handbook.
For additional information, including a list of
Primary Care Providers
, visit the
PartnershipHP
.org
website.
For additional information about Covered California Health Plans, including income tax credits and cost-sharing reductions to reduce premiums and out-of-pocket health costs, visit
CoveredCA
.com
County Medical Insurance Program (CMSP)
CMSP provides health coverage for Shasta County residents, ages 21 through 64, with incomes below established limits and who are not eligible to Medi-Cal benefits.
What does CMSP cover?
A
detailed list of covered services
is available on the CMSP website.
How to apply for CMSP
All individuals must first apply for Medi-Cal.  If determined ineligible for Medi-Cal, an application for CMSP will be provided by mail with instructions on how to apply.
Community Health Advocates (
CHAs
) are available to help!
If you are having difficulty with paperwork or need help applying for services,
CHAs
are available in all our
offices
. They can help with
CalFresh
and Medi-Cal applications and identify community resources to assist with utilities, housing, transportation, etc.
Para
assistencia
en
español
,
llame
530-229-8391 o 530-335-6701.
